title: celtis occidentalis var. canina
intro: Celtis occidentalis var. canina, also known as the American hackberry or canina hackberry, is a variety of the hackberry tree native to North America, characterized by its small, yellowish-green flowers and edible fruit. The plant's roots, bark, and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The medicinal properties of Celtis occidentalis var. canina are believed to provide relief from fever, sore throats, and digestive issues, as well as to boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting overall well-being.
